sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After clicking the hollow square, the first two N objects in the top grid disappeared! The G frame and hollow square are gone too. So the mechanic is:
1. Click on an N → G frame appears around it, hollow square appears somewhere
2. Click on the hollow square → The N objects to the left of the hollow square's position get removed
Wait, let me look more carefully. Before clicking the hollow square (step 26):
- Top grid had 4 N objects with a G frame around the first one and a hollow square between positions 2 and 3
After clicking (step 27):
- Top grid now has only 2 N objects remaining (the ones that were at positions 3 and 4)
- The first two N positions are now just gray squares
So clicking the hollow square removes the N objects that are to the left of where the hollow square was positioned. The hollow square was between positions 2 and 3, and it removed positions 1 and 2.
Wait, but that doesn't make complete sense. Let me reconsider. The hollow square was at col 25-28, which is between the 2nd N (col 19-22) and 3rd N (col 31-34). After clicking, the first two Ns are gone.
So the mechanic seems to be:
1. Click an N → marks it with a G frame
2. A hollow square appears somewhere (maybe at the next N's position or between Ns)
3. Click the hollow square → removes all Ns up to and including the hollow square's position
Actually, let me reconsider. The hollow square appeared at col 25-28. The Ns were at cols 13-16, 19-22, 31-34, 43-46. The hollow square was between the 2nd and 3rd N. After clicking, the first two Ns disappeared.
So maybe the mechanic is:
1. Click an N to "select" it (G frame appears)
2. A hollow square appears at a specific position
3. Click the hollow square to "cut" - removing all Ns from the selected one up to (but not including) the hollow square's position
Or maybe: clicking the hollow square removes all Ns that are to the left of the hollow square.
Let me verify by trying another sequence. I'll click on one of the remaining Ns and see what happens.
Actually wait - looking at the images more carefully:
- Step 25 (before any clicks): 4 Ns in top grid, 1 N in bottom grid
- Step 26 (after clicking first N): G frame around first N, hollow square appears
- Step 27 (after clicking hollow square): First 2 Ns gone, only 2 Ns remain in top grid
So the hollow square was at position between 2nd and 3rd N, and clicking it removed the first 2 Ns.
Let me try clicking on one of the remaining Ns to understand the pattern better.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state after clicking hollow square
after = current_frame.segmentation
print("=== N objects now ===")
for n in after['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, row={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, col={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

print("\n=== G/g objects ===")
for n in after['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('G', 'g'):
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, row={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, col={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Click on the next N (the one at col 31-34, row 15-18)
# Center would be around row 16, col 32
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 32}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

  id=24, row=15-18, col=43-46, pixels=12
  id=49, row=51-54, col=43-46, pixels=12
